events.src: Map podcast · Felix Riisberg (Anthropic head of product)
events.text: Riisberg (head of product, leads eng on Claude Co-Work): Claude Mythos Preview is "a dramatic step up" internally, "particularly good at finding security issues" — it generated Project Glasswing; Mythos to be kept "completely closed and private." Co-Work extends agentic capability to non-coding knowledge work; memory lives in the harness (markdown "skills"), roadmap ≤1 month. Jensen Huang (same source): Anthropic's "$5-6 billion" month in February as an "Oppenheimer moment."

Allen Roush (AI-coding call): ~80% of business revenue is token-based (not subscription); enterprise API by token is profitable while consumer subs ($20/$200) lose money. Chronic compute constraints cause persistent outages ("models down all the time").

Hyperscaler recap: Google books the gross of the Broadcom→Google→Anthropic TPU resale (markup ~10-15% / ~$2-3k per chip). Anthropic TPU orders: ~1 GW in 2026 (initial $10bn + Broadcom order $11bn end-26 = ~$21bn) + ~3 GW incremental for 2027; Google also provides credit backstops for third-party data centers.

BofA/Arya: OpenAI + Anthropic together ~40-50% of the major hyperscalers' backlog; Anthropic "always scrambling for more capacity" — service "slows down in the middle of the day." Arya expects NVDA will want to invest directly in Anthropic (an earlier "regret"); Vera CPUs delivered to Oracle, SpaceX, OpenAI and Anthropic.

Jefferies (Brian): a narrative inversion — "Sam had customers and usage and Dario didn't... Dario was projecting numbers from a ~$2bn ARR company. Now Dario is [a] $30 billion ARR company." Anthropic did a "$50 billion Fluidstack deal" still with ARR ~$1-2bn — one of the most aggressively sized compute deals vs usage.

UBS (Arcuri): Anthropic's XPU commitment with Broadcom is 5 GW, all Broadcom (no MediaTek); the Anthropic + OpenAI SPV points to ~20 GW. Broadcom frames OpenAI's compute demand as similar to Anthropic's, but with lower revenue visibility — Anthropic is the highest-visibility ASIC anchor.

FluidStack expert (WFC): despite serving ~30 models (half proprietary/half open), ~95% of tokens served are Claude/Anthropic — concrete evidence of frontier capture. Anthropic's Q1-2026 inflection was the single biggest driver of the GPU supply-demand imbalance; Colossus capacity now sold for inference to Google and Anthropic.

BG2Pod: Fable 5 (Jun 2026) is SOTA on nearly every benchmark, 1M-token context, always-on adaptive thinking; Karpathy flagged long-running task capability as the real differentiator. xAI/SpaceX monetizes a compute deal with Anthropic at ~$22-23B/GW/year. Frontier captures ~90% of the economic value even with open-source capturing the majority of tokens.

JPM: revenue run-rate "fast-approaching $50B" (from ~$9B at YE2025); ~10+ GW of multi-vendor commitments. Capacity: AWS up to 5 GW ($100B+ over 10 years, Trainium); Google up to 1M TPUs / >1 GW by 2026; Azure up to 1 GW ($30B); and a Broadcom deal for multiple GW of next-gen TPUs ($35B, Google backstops the lease payments). Trains/serves on >1M Trainium2 chips (Project Rainier).

Cantor: Anthropic signed only a small ~$2-3bn / 70 MW deal with CoreWeave (~Apr 10-11) — read as well below true need; Cantor expects a sizable CoreWeave-Anthropic deal (~$20-30bn) alongside a big RPO beat in 2Q. Google↔SpaceX deal (~$10/GPU-hr H100) read as channeling Colossus capacity to Anthropic.

Big Technology w/ Ramp: Anthropic is the most-used AI vendor among US businesses (~41% of firms vs OpenAI ~39.5%), having overtaken OpenAI in May 2026. AI is the fastest-growing cost category Ramp has ever tracked. Bear: neither Anthropic nor OpenAI offers spend-management controls, the #1 enterprise complaint.
BTG (Mythos expert): Mythos (cyber-capable) remains unreleased — too dangerous/compute-heavy to serve broadly.

Bernstein: Anthropic's >$100B deal with AWS is excluded from AWS's reported $364B backlog (i.e., AWS backlog understates Anthropic's committed compute); Anthropic appears for the first time (alongside OpenAI) in the CY25 Gartner PaaS data — evidence that the private labs already have the scale to register as cloud/PaaS players.

StoneX/TrendForce (Avril): an Anthropic-Micron partnership combines server DRAM + enterprise SSD in 2027 + HBM in 2028 — the HBM component implies Anthropic is developing its own in-house ASIC (you only need HBM if you buy/build accelerators). Anthropic is pursuing the same deal structure with SK Hynix and Samsung.
